title: Reading and Use of English – Part 8
description: You are going to read an article about four people who participated in a skill-sharing community. For questions 47–56, choose from the people A–D. The people may be chosen more than once.

texts.B:
### Julian, The Language-for-Cooking Exchange
'I taught Spanish in exchange for Italian pastry lessons,' Julian explains. [51]The most challenging part was the initial frustration of my student, who struggled with basic grammar while I was eager to start baking.[/51] [48]I was surprised to find that my own teaching skills in my day job as a corporate trainer improved because I had to simplify everything.[/48] [53]I learned that communication is 30% words and 70% shared passion and gesture.[/53] [50]I realized that my background in linguistics helped me identify the exact phonetic hurdles my partner was facing.[/50] [47]Initially, the heat and noise of a busy kitchen were quite a shock to my usual quiet office routine.[/47] It was a delicious cultural bridge.
texts.C:
### Sarah, The Web Design-for-Yoga Swap
Sarah built a website for a yoga studio in exchange for private classes. [49]The experience taught me that the value of a service isn't always reflected in its market price, but in its personal impact.[/49] [52]I hadn't anticipated the deep sense of calm I’d gain from the physical practice, which saved me from burnout.[/52] [47]I was quite startled by how much the lack of a traditional contract made me feel anxious and vulnerable in the first week.[/47] [54]What I valued most was the spiritual guidance of the instructor, who helped me reframe my relationship with work.[/54] [56]If I hadn't received a recommendation from a mutual friend, I’d never have trusted the arrangement.[/56] It was a holistic success.
texts.D:
### Victor, The Carpentry-for-Gardening Trade
Victor exchanged furniture repair for help with his vegetable plot. [51]We struggled with the timing; his plants needed attention in spring, while my workshop was busiest in winter.[/51] [48]I noticed that my general physical stamina and back health improved significantly, which was a great side effect I hadn't planned for.[/48] [53]I realized that a garden is a slow-motion conversation with nature that requires constant attention.[/53] [50]I found that the precision I used in my previous career as a machinist was perfect for the delicate grafting of fruit trees.[/50] [55]It took a while to adjust to the muddy reality of outdoor work, which I found quite repulsive at first.[/55] [52]I’d initially worried he wouldn't like my craft style.
texts.A:
### Elena, The Music-for-Photography Deal
'I taught the cello in exchange for professional portraits,' Elena laughs. [49]The project made me realize that we all have 'hidden capital' that we often undervalue in ourselves.[/49] [56]I wouldn't have found the partner if I hadn't attended a local networking event for independent artists.[/56] [54]I was moved by the camaraderie that developed; we ended up collaborating on a joint art project for the city.[/54] [47]I hadn't expected the physical exhaustion of long photo shoots to be so draining initially.[/47] [55]I found the initial task of explaining musical theory to a visual thinker incredibly frustrating and difficult.[/55] [52]I’d been concerned that our professional levels wouldn't match, but the balance was perfect.

questions.question: mentions being surprised by the physical or environmental impact of the new activity?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) mentions the heat/noise of the kitchen, Sarah (B) the lack of a contract (psychological), and Elena (D) the exhaustion of photo shoots.

questions.question: notes a positive change in their professional skills or physical health as a side effect?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) saw his corporate training skills improve, and Victor (C) improved his stamina and back health.

questions.question: expresses a change in their perspective on the value of talent or services?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) reflects on value vs. market price, and Elena (D) on 'hidden capital'.

questions.question: mentions how their professional background or skills were advantageous to the swap?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) used his linguistics background, and Victor (C) used his machinist precision.

questions.question: describes a challenge related to timing or the partner's learning process?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) mentions his student's frustration with grammar, and Victor (C) the mismatch in seasonal busy periods.

questions.question: admits to having had prior concerns or having gained an unexpected mental benefit?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) gained a sense of calm that prevented burnout, and Elena (D) worried about professional levels matching.

questions.question: reflects on a broader philosophical lesson learned about communication or nature?
questions.explanation: Julian (A) learned about non-verbal communication, and Victor (C) saw gardening as a conversation with nature.

questions.question: highlights the value of the guidance or the collaborative spirit that emerged?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) valued the spiritual guidance, and Elena (D) the camaraderie and joint project.

questions.question: describes a period of frustration or adjustment to the physical or intellectual demands?
questions.explanation: Victor (C) adjusted to the mud, and Elena (D) found explaining theory to a visual thinker difficult.

questions.question: acknowledges the specific external factor or event that facilitated the connection?
questions.explanation: Sarah (B) mentions a friend's recommendation, and Elena (D) a networking event.
